reason: The remediation only edits /etc/gdm/custom.conf to disable automatic login, a standard STIG hardening step. It does not remove essential services or credentials and poses minimal risk of lockout. GDM will simply require user authentication on the next graphical login.
estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; Potential mis‑syntax in custom.conf could prevent GDM from starting; If the file does not exist, the script creates/updates it which may affect custom configurations
